_comment: Isolated morale-recovery scenario (#657): single, very-weak infantry (morale 3, 40 soldiers) faces a fast cavalry charge from distance. On contact, the infantry's low morale triggers immediate rout. The cavalry's charge momentum carries it past the routed target; by the time it turns to pursue again, the infantry has gained distance and recovers in isolation. Demonstrates a routing unit rallying when the attacker re-engages too slowly. Camera frames both units initially, then pans to follow the routing infantry's recovery.
